content.full_text:
January 30, 1990

Kevin Braaten-Moen
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: CONFIRMATION OF TELEPHONE ADVICE; LOBBYING DISCLOSURE

Dear Mr. Moen:

This letter will confirm telephone advice which you provided to me last week. We spoke on two occasions regarding a luncheon. I presented to you two different factual circumstances. I will set forth each below.

Situation 1 - Ten lobbyists get together for lunch with an agency official. The lunch is arranged by one lobbyist, who invites the other lobbyists and the agency official. Total cost of the luncheon, including tip (everyone is served the same thing) is $149.16. Thus the prorata cost is $13.56 per person. The agency official reimburses by personal check for $3.56, bringing the gift within the $10 limit. The lobbyists split the tab 10 ways (each pays $14.92). Thus, each lobbyist paid $1.00 toward the agency official's $10 gift.

Situation 2 - All facts are the same except that the arranging lobbyist pays for the $10 portion of the agency official's lunch and the other 9 lobbyists each pays only $13.56, his or her prorata share. The 9 lobbyists have paid nothing toward the agency official's meal.

I inquired regarding the reporting requirements under each of these two situations for one of the nine lobbyists who did not arrange the luncheon. You provided the following advice:

Situation 1 - The non-arranging lobbyists would have to report the luncheon as an activity expense, showing the payment which benefits the agency official as $10, with a note that they paid $1.00 of that total.

Situation 2 - The non-arranging lobbyists would not have to report the luncheon as an activity expense since they did not arrange the luncheon and paid nothing toward the agency official's gift. On the other hand, the

Kevin Braaten-Moen  
January 31, 1990  
Page 2

arranging lobbyist would have to report the full $10 amount as the gift.

I would appreciate it if you could confirm this advice in writing for my records.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

ROBERT E. LEIDIGH

REL/k1
